mission.title: Install Konveyor (Tackle) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Konveyor is an open-source application modernization platform that helps developers and platform teams analyze, assess, and transform legacy applications for Kubernetes. The Tackle toolkit is deployed via the tackle2-operator, which manages the lifecycle of Konveyor components including the Hub API, UI, and analysis engines.

steps.title: Install OLM (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Konveyor's operator is distributed via OLM (Operator Lifecycle Manager). If OLM is not already installed on your cluster (it is pre-installed on OpenShift):
```bash
curl -sL https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-lifecycle-manager/releases/download/v0.28.0/install.sh | bash -s v0.28.0
```
Verify OLM is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n olm
```

steps.title: Install the Konveyor Operator
steps.description:
Apply the Konveyor operator manifest which creates the namespace, operator group, catalog source, and subscription:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/konveyor/tackle2-operator/main/tackle-k8s.yaml
```
This creates the `konveyor-tackle` namespace and installs the operator via OLM.

steps.title: Wait for the operator to be ready
steps.description:
Wait for the operator pod to be running in the `konveyor-tackle` namespace:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=300s deployment/tackle-operator -n konveyor-tackle
```
Or check manually:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n konveyor-tackle
```

steps.title: Create the Tackle custom resource
steps.description:
Create a Tackle CR to trigger the deployment of all Konveyor components (Hub, UI, Pathfinder, Keycloak, PostgreSQL):
```yaml
apiVersion: tackle.konveyor.io/v1alpha1
kind: Tackle
metadata:
  name: tackle
  namespace: konveyor-tackle
spec: {}
```
Apply it:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: tackle.konveyor.io/v1alpha1
kind: Tackle
metadata:
  name: tackle
  namespace: konveyor-tackle
spec: {}
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Wait for all Konveyor components to be running (this may take several minutes as images are pulled):
```bash
kubectl get pods -n konveyor-tackle
```
You should see pods for: tackle-hub, tackle-ui, tackle-keycloak, tackle-postgresql, and the operator.

Access the UI via port-forward:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/tackle-ui -n konveyor-tackle 8080:8080
```
Then open http://localhost:8080 in your browser.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show all Konveyor (Tackle) pods running in the konveyor-tackle namespace. The UI is accessible via port-forward on port 8080. Default credentials are admin/password (from Keycloak).
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: tackle.konveyor.io/v1alpha1
kind: Tackle
metadata:
  name: tackle
  namespace: konveyor-tackle
spec: {}
EOF

uninstall.title: Delete the Tackle CR
uninstall.description:
Remove the Tackle custom resource which will clean up all managed components:
```bash
kubectl delete tackle tackle -n konveyor-tackle
```

uninstall.title: Remove the operator and namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the operator subscription and namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/konveyor/tackle2-operator/main/tackle-k8s.yaml
```
Clean up any remaining CRDs:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep konveyor | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep tackle | awk '{print $1}')
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via OLM
upgrade.description:
OLM handles operator upgrades automatically via the subscription. To check the current version:
```bash
kubectl get csv -n konveyor-tackle
```
To trigger a manual upgrade, update the subscription channel or approve a pending install plan:
```bash
kubectl get installplan -n konveyor-tackle
```

troubleshooting.title: OLM not installed
troubleshooting.description:
If you see errors about missing CatalogSource or Subscription resources, OLM is not installed. Install it first:
```bash
curl -sL https://github.com/operator-framework/operator-lifecycle-manager/releases/download/v0.28.0/install.sh | bash -s v0.28.0
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
Konveyor requires significant resources. If pods are stuck in Pending:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n konveyor-tackle
```
Check for insufficient CPU/memory or missing PVCs. Ensure your cluster has a default StorageClass for PostgreSQL.

troubleshooting.title: Tackle CR not reconciling
troubleshooting.description:
If creating the Tackle CR doesn't spawn any pods, check the operator logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -n konveyor-tackle deployment/tackle-operator
```
Ensure the operator pod is running and the Tackle CRD exists:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep tackle
```

troubleshooting.title: UI not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If the UI is not loading after port-forward:
1. Verify the tackle-ui pod is running
2. Check the service exists: `kubectl get svc tackle-ui -n konveyor-tackle`
3. Check UI pod logs: `kubectl logs -n konveyor-tackle -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tackle-ui`

prerequisites.description: Kubernetes 1.22+ cluster with OLM installed (pre-installed on OpenShift). A default StorageClass is required for PostgreSQL persistence. kubectl must be configured to access your cluster with cluster-admin permissions.
